questions.q: Who founded the Raman Research Institute?
questions.explanation: The Raman Research Institute was founded by the Nobel laureate physicist Sir C. V. Raman.

questions.q: In which Indian city is the Raman Research Institute located?
questions.explanation: The Raman Research Institute is located in Bengaluru, Karnataka.

questions.q: In which year was the Raman Research Institute founded?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman founded the institute in 1948.

questions.q: What is the main field of research pursued at the Raman Research Institute?
questions.explanation: The institute was established to pursue fundamental research in physics.

questions.q: C. V. Raman is most famous for discovering which phenomenon?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman discovered the scattering of light known as the Raman effect.

questions.q: For which discovery did C. V. Raman win the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Raman won the 1930 Nobel Prize in Physics for his work on the scattering of light and the effect named after him.

questions.q: In which year did C. V. Raman win the Nobel Prize in Physics?
questions.explanation: Raman received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930.

questions.q: C. V. Raman was the first person of which background to win a Nobel Prize in a science?
questions.explanation: Raman was the first Asian to win a Nobel Prize in any of the sciences.

questions.q: What everyday phenomenon did C. V. Raman famously study that reportedly inspired his light-scattering work?
options: The colour of the sea and sky
questions.explanation: Raman's curiosity about the blue colour of the sea led to his investigations of light scattering.

questions.q: C. V. Raman received which of India's highest civilian honours in 1954?
questions.explanation: Raman was among the first recipients of the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian award, in 1954.

questions.q: On which date is National Science Day celebrated in India, marking the announcement of Raman's discovery?
questions.explanation: National Science Day is observed on 28 February, commemorating Raman's discovery of the Raman effect in 1928.

questions.q: In which year did Raman first announce his discovery of the Raman effect?
questions.explanation: Raman announced his discovery of the inelastic scattering of light in 1928.

questions.q: The Raman effect involves what kind of scattering of light?
questions.explanation: The Raman effect is the inelastic scattering of light, in which scattered photons gain or lose energy.

questions.q: Which organisation primarily funds and administers the Raman Research Institute today?
questions.explanation: The Raman Research Institute is now an autonomous institute funded by the Department of Science and Technology, Government of India.

questions.q: Before founding the Raman Research Institute, C. V. Raman did much of his prize-winning work in which city?
questions.explanation: Raman carried out his famous light-scattering research in Calcutta at the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science.

questions.q: C. V. Raman served as the first Indian director of which Bengaluru institution before establishing his own institute?
questions.explanation: Raman was the first Indian director of the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) in Bengaluru.

questions.q: The Raman effect is the basis for which widely used analytical technique?
questions.explanation: Raman spectroscopy, used to identify molecules by their vibrational signatures, is based on the Raman effect.

questions.q: Which young physicist collaborated with C. V. Raman in the experiments confirming the Raman effect?
questions.explanation: K. S. Krishnan worked closely with Raman during the experiments that revealed the Raman effect.

questions.q: What kind of light source did Raman primarily use in his original light-scattering experiments?
questions.explanation: Raman initially used filtered sunlight focused by lenses; lasers had not yet been invented in 1928.

questions.q: The Raman Research Institute is known for research in which of these areas in addition to optics?
questions.explanation: The institute conducts research in fields such as astronomy and astrophysics alongside light and matter physics.

questions.q: C. V. Raman was the uncle of which other Nobel Prize-winning physicist?
questions.explanation: Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar, who won the 1983 Nobel Prize in Physics, was C. V. Raman's nephew.

questions.q: What is the full name behind the initials 'C. V.' in C. V. Raman?
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman stood for Chandrasekhara Venkata Raman.

questions.q: In Raman spectroscopy, scattered light shifted to lower energy than the incident light produces which lines?
questions.explanation: Stokes lines arise when scattered photons lose energy to the molecule, appearing at lower frequency than the incident light.

questions.q: Which European scientist had theoretically predicted such inelastic light scattering before Raman observed it experimentally?
questions.explanation: Adolf Smekal predicted the inelastic scattering of light in 1923, several years before Raman's experimental confirmation.

questions.q: Soviet physicists Landsberg and Mandelstam independently observed inelastic light scattering in 1928 in which material?
questions.explanation: Grigory Landsberg and Leonid Mandelstam observed the effect in quartz crystals around the same time as Raman.

questions.q: Besides light scattering, C. V. Raman did notable acoustical research on which family of instruments?
questions.explanation: Early in his career Raman studied the acoustics of musical instruments such as the violin, tabla and mridangam.

questions.q: Which scientific society, closely tied to Raman's early career, did he lead, and whose journal he edited?
questions.explanation: Raman founded the Indian Academy of Sciences in 1934 and served as its president, basing it in Bengaluru.

questions.q: C. V. Raman was elected a Fellow of which prestigious British scientific body in 1924?
questions.explanation: Raman was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) in 1924, before his Nobel-winning discovery.

questions.q: Later in life at his institute, Raman studied the optical properties and colours of which gemstones in detail?
questions.explanation: At the Raman Research Institute, Raman extensively studied the optics, structure and colours of diamonds and other crystals and minerals.

questions.q: What magnitude order is the intensity of Raman-scattered light compared with the much stronger elastic scattering of light?
options: Vastly weaker, only a tiny fraction of scattered photons
questions.explanation: Raman scattering is extremely weak, typically involving only about one in a million scattered photons, which made its detection a remarkable achievement.
